segments: Consumer (PCs, tablets, voice and music, XR)
Edge Networking (mobile broadband, wireless access points)
Industrial (handhelds, retail, tracking and logistics, utilities)

dependencies: Limited number of third-party suppliers for semiconductor manufacturing
Concentration of foundry suppliers in Asia-Pacific region, primarily Taiwan and Korea
Single-source or limited-source suppliers for certain products
Dependence on TSMC for leading-edge process technology nodes
Primary warehouses for finished goods located in Singapore
Significant engineering workforce based in India
risks: Supply chain concentration in Asia-Pacific region
Geopolitical tensions involving China and Taiwan could disrupt chipset supply from Taiwan
Limited manufacturing capacity at leading-edge technology nodes
Supplier allocation of capacity to competitors
Natural disasters, climate change effects, geopolitical conflicts affecting supplier facilities
Trade and national security policies limiting transactions with certain suppliers
Cyber-attacks on supplier IT systems and manufacturing facilities
Raw material shortages and price increases
Extended manufacturing lead-times increasing inventory risk
Periodic supply/capacity constraints in semiconductor industry

regulatory_risks: Antitrust and competition law investigations and proceedings
Patent licensing practice challenges under FRAND commitments
Trade protection and national security policies (U.S. and China)
Export control regulations (U.S. Export Administration Regulations)
Tariffs and import/export restrictions
Standards Development Organizations (SDOs) intellectual property policy changes
European Commission proposed regulations on standard-essential patents
Cybersecurity laws requiring IT security certifications in various jurisdictions
Environmental, health and safety regulations (including climate change regulations)
Greenhouse gas emissions regulations and net-zero commitments
Privacy and data protection laws (varying by jurisdiction)
AI technology regulations (emerging regulatory landscape)
Automotive industry safety, security and quality standards
Supply chain and responsible sourcing regulations (including conflict minerals)
Corporate governance and ESG reporting requirements
Tax law changes (including FDII deduction changes, research and development capitalization requirements)
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax framework (15% minimum tax)
Foreign withholding tax regulations (particularly in Korea)
Spectrum availability and license issuance regulations
Labor and employment regulations across multiple jurisdictions

sanction_risks: U.S. government restrictions on transactions with Chinese companies (e.g., Huawei export license revoked)
Chinese government policies limiting or preventing transactions with U.S. companies
U.S./China trade tensions impacting ability to sell products to Chinese customers
Chinese government Made in China 2025 campaign encouraging domestic semiconductor development
Potential U.S. or Chinese government actions limiting cross-border technology transfer
Restrictions on selling products containing Chinese-origin content in the United States
Chinese government policies regulating capital outflows affecting payment timing from licensees
Geopolitical conflict between China and Taiwan threatening chipset supply from Taiwan foundries
U.S. Department of Commerce Entity List restrictions
Foreign government support of competing technologies or OEMs
Trade protection policies including tariffs on semiconductors and devices
National security policies restricting technology sales to certain countries or companies
